blurb: The Centre for Emerging Technology and Security (CETaS) is a research centre at the Alan Turing Institute focused on UK national security and emerging technology, producing some of the sharpest UK-government-facing AI-security analysis.

publications.title: The Next Frontier: Security Implications of Future AI Paradigms
publications.byline: Ardi Janjeva, Sylvester Kaczmarek, Angus Shennan and Carolyn Ashurst
publications.summary: CETaS Research Report mapping the emergence of novel AI paradigms (alternative architectures, data sources, learning methods, hardware substrates) and assessing the national-security implications of the next wave of disruptive AI for the UK.

publications.title: International AI Safety Report 2026
publications.byline: Chaired by Yoshua Bengio (100+ independent experts)
publications.summary: Second edition of the International AI Safety Report — the comprehensive expert review of the capabilities and risks of general-purpose AI, chaired from the Turing Institute. A flagship reference document for UK and international AI governance.

publications.title: CETaS Outlook: Emerging Technology Trends to Watch in 2026
publications.byline: James Lloyd, Ardi Janjeva and Sam Stockwell
publications.summary: CETaS Expert Analysis forecasting 2026 as the year AI becomes more embedded in the physical world, more autonomous, and more entwined with geopolitics — a horizon-scan for UK security policy.

publications.title: Asia-Pacific AI Governance: A Call for Middle-Power Partnerships
publications.summary: CETaS Expert Analysis (May 2026) arguing for middle-power partnerships in Asia-Pacific AI governance. Exact day-level date not confirmed from a primary page (CETaS site is Cloudflare-gated); month (May 2026) is from CETaS's own citation string. Direct publication URL not captured — links to the publications index.

notes: Harvested 2026-06-27 via web_search after the live site (cetas.turing.ac.uk) hard-Cloudflare-blocked both the headless browser (interactive turnstile) AND web_fetch (403 'Just a moment'). Titles, authors and series are from CETaS's OWN citation strings as indexed by search; dates: 'The Next Frontier' (30 Apr 2026) and 'Tech Trends 2026' (23 Jan 2026) from search-result metadata; the International AI Safety Report's 3 Feb 2026 date is CROSS-CONFIRMED by three independent sources (internationalaisafetyreport.org, Lexology, Princeton CITP). The Asia-Pacific item has only a month-level date (May 2026) and no captured deep-link, so date=null and url points to the index — honest rather than invented. Recommend swapping these to live first-party deep-links + day-level dates once a turnstile-capable fetch path exists.
